member.summary: Defines how long (in ms) loot will be available for picking up on the map.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.LootOptions.ItemOwnershipTime
member.summary: Defines how long (in ms) an item drop will be ''owned'' by a player and their party.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.LootOptions.ShowUnownedItems
member.summary: Defines whether players can see items they do not ''own'' on the map.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.LootOptions.ConsolidateMapDrops
member.summary: Defines whether spawning items on the map from anything but the map attribute should consolidate it into one item. When false, will drop multiple items with a quantity of 1, rather than a single item with a higher quantity for non-stackable items.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.LootOptions.EnableLootWindow
member.summary: Configures whether the loot window feature of the client is allowed to be active. Note, there's no such setting on the client so enabling it here enables it there.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.LootOptions.MaximumLootWindowItems
member.summary: Configures the maximum amount of items to be displayed on the client's Loot Window.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.LootOptions.MaximumLootWindowDistance
member.summary: Configures the maximum amount of tiles around you to search for loot with the Loot Window.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.LootOptions.IndividualizedLootAutoIncludePartyMembers
member.summary: When killing an NPC which spawns individualized loot for all of its attackers, include loot for all their party members, even if they didn't participate (deal damage) to the npc

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.AllowResetRadius
member.summary: If NPCs are allowed to reset after moving out of a specified radius when starting to fight another entity.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.ResetRadius
member.summary: Configures the radius in which an NPC is allowed to move after starting to fight another entity.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.AllowNewResetLocationBeforeFinish
member.summary: If the NPC is allowed to gain a new reset center point while it is still busy moving to its original reset point. NOTE: Can be used to allow the NPCs to be dragged very far away, as it constantly resets the center of its radius!!!

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.ResetVitalsAndStatuses
member.summary: If the NPC should completely restore its vitals and statusses once it starts resetting.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.ContinuouslyResetVitalsAndStatuses
member.summary: If the NPCs health should continue to reset to full and clear statuses while working its way to the reset location

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.AllowEngagingWhileResetting
member.summary: If true, a NPC can be attacked while they are resetting. Their new attacker will become a target if they are within the reset radius

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.ShowLevelByName
member.summary: If the level of an NPC is shown next to their name.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.IntangibleDuringReset
member.summary: If true, NPCs that are resetting will walk through players

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.NpcOptions.ResetIfCombatTimerExceeded
member.summary: If true, NPCs will go to reset state if their combat timer is exceeded

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketOptions.BatchPlayerMovementPackets
member.summary: If this value is true, player movements will be sent in the batch with npc/event movement when maps update instead of in realtime which will reduce workload on the network pool at the expense of being slightly delayed

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketOptions.BatchActionMessagePackets
member.summary: If this value is true, action messages will be sent in a batch maps update instead of in realtime which will reduce workload on the network pool at the expense of being slightly delayed

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketOptions.BatchAnimationPackets
member.summary: If this value is true, animation packetswill be sent in a batch maps update instead of in realtime which will reduce workload on the network pool at the expense of being slightly delayed

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.MinimumPing
member.summary: Assumed minimum ping a client will have when communicating with the server.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.ErrorMarginFactor
member.summary: This factor is multiplied by the client ping in order to determine the acceptable error margin in packet timing.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.NaturalLowerMargin
member.summary: Lower bounds of adjusted packet times with pings taken into account for packets to be considered natural.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.NaturalUpperMargin
member.summary: Upper bounds of adjusted packet times with pings taken into account for packets to be considered natural.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.AllowedSpikePackets
member.summary: Number of consecutive unnatural packets that are accepted in short intervals before being dropped.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.BaseDesyncForegiveness
member.summary: The base amount of time in ms that we will forgive the client for being desyned

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.DesyncForgivenessFactor
member.summary: A factor in whch we will allow the client time to shift from the servers before dropping packets with configured error margins taken into account.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.DesyncForgivenessInterval
member.summary: A value measured in milliseconds in which a packet that is out of sync will be forgiven (no matter how out of sync) and timing will sent to the client.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.EditorThresholds
member.summary: Packet flooding detection thresholds for the game editor. (No Restrictions.)

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.PlayerThresholds
member.summary: Packet flooding detection thresholds for general players. Pretty strict. Might need to be adjusted if there is a lot of high paced actions/movement/combat in your game.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.ModAdminThresholds
member.summary: Packet flooding detection thresholds for mods/admins. Hopefully you trust these guys. Limits need to be higher than general players since they can warp quickly around the world with shift click.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PacketSecurityOptions.DefaultThresholds
member.summary: Packet flooding detection threshholds for all users who are not yet logged in.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PartyOptions.MaximumMembers
member.summary: Defines the maximum amount of members a party can have.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.PartyOptions.BonusExperiencePercentPerMember
member.summary: It will determine if you will have an XP bonus for each member, Eg 10, will give 10% more to the monster's total XP, then split equally to all members. A monster that would give 100 XP, now gives 120, getting 60 for each member, when 2 in the party.

member.summary: Number of bank slots a player has.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.CpsLock
member.summary: If the server logic thread sleeps after each loop iteration by default.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MaxLogicThreads
member.summary: Maximum number threads that will be created to handle map logic, npc/event updates, hots/dots/status effects, projectiles while the server is under load.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.LogicThreadIdleTimeout
member.summary: This is how long (in ms) a logic thread in our logic pool should be idle before it is considered unneeded and therefore disposed.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MinNetworkThreads
member.summary: Minimum number of threads that will be used for packet processing which we don't want to slow down our game loop.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MaxNetworkThreads
member.summary: Maximum number of threads that will be used for packet processing which we don't want to slow down our game loop.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.NetworkThreadIdleTimeout
member.summary: This is how long (in ms) a network thread in our network pool should be idle before it is considered unneeded and therefore disposed.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MinDatabaseThreads
member.summary: Minimum number of threads that will be used for database interactions like player or variable saving

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MaxDatabaseThreads
member.summary: Maximum number of threads that will be used for database interactions like player or variable saving

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.DatabaseThreadIdleTimeout
member.summary: This is how long (in ms) a database thread should be idle before it is considered unneeded and therefore disposed.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.DatabaseSaveServerVariablesInterval
member.summary: How often should the server save changes to server variable values?

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MinSystemThreadpoolWorkerThreads
member.summary: Minimum number of worker threads that will be used in the system managed threadpool (-1 for default)

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MaxSystemThreadpoolWorkerThreads
member.summary: Maximum number of worker threads that will be used in the system managed threadpool (-1 for default)

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MinSystemThreadpoolIOThreads
member.summary: Minimum number of worker threads that will be used for io the system managed threadpool (-1 for default)

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MaxSystemThreadpoolIOThreads
member.summary: Maximum number of worker threads that will be used for io the system managed threadpool (-1 for default)

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.MapUpdateInterval
member.summary: This controls how often maps/npcs should be updated in ms.

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.ProjectileUpdateInterval
member.summary: This controls how often projectiles should updated in ms. (It is recommended to keep this the same value as the MapUpdateInterval so they are all updated at the same time)

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.PlayerSaveInterval
member.summary: How long to delay between player saves (default 2 min)

member.name: P:Intersect.Config.ProcessingOptions.CommonEventAutorunStartInterval
member.summary: How often should the server try to execute autorun common events for a player? This can get expensive if you have a lot of autorun common events with lots of complex conditions.

member.summary: How often should the server check for stale cooldowns on players to remove them from their respective collections.

member.name: M:Intersect.Plugins.Interfaces.IPacketHelper.TryRegisterPacketType``1
member.summary: Try to add a packet type to the registry.

member.name: M:Intersect.Plugins.Interfaces.IPacketHelper.TryRegisterPacketPreprocessor``2(``0@)
member.summary:
Try to add a packet preprocessor (multiple allowed) to the packet handler registry for the specified packet type.
Preprocessors are meant as boolean filters for whether a packet should or should be handled (pre-hook, handler, post-hook), and should also be where any packet mutation occurs (if a packet needs to be mutated for some reason).
Preprocessors run before all handlers and pre-hooks.

member.name: M:Intersect.Plugins.Interfaces.IPacketHelper.TryRegisterPacketHandler``2(``0@)
member.summary: Try to add a packet handler (can only be one per packet) to the packet handler registry for the specified packet type.

member.name: M:Intersect.Plugins.Interfaces.IPacketHelper.TryRegisterPacketPreHook``2(``0@)
member.summary:
Try to add a packet pre-hook (multiple allowed) to the packet handler registry for the specified packet type.
Pre-hooks run immediately before handlers.

member.name: M:Intersect.Plugins.Interfaces.IPacketHelper.TryRegisterPacketPostHook``2(``0@)
member.summary:
Try to add a packet post-hook (multiple allowed) to the packet handler registry for the specified packet type.
Post-hooks run immediately after handlers.
